item.title: Alex de Minaur loses to Hamad Medjedovic at Barcelona Open
item.description: Alex de Minaur's uneven season has hit another road bump as Australia's number one succumbed to a shock last-16 loss at the Barcelona Open.

item.title: Four-year-old boy dies in hospital after being hit by vehicle on Dubbo street
item.description: A four-year-old boy has died in hospital after being hit by a car on a suburban street in Dubbo.

item.title: Arsenal draws with Sporting, Bayern Munich defeats Real Madrid in UEFA Champions League
item.description: Arsenal advance to the UEFA Champions League semifinals with a goalless draw, as Bayern Munich defeat Real Madrid.

item.title: Analysis of 1,300 new dinosaur bones overhauls understanding of Qld fossil emblem
item.description: The dinosaur's face lift has become the butt of online jokes among palaeontology fans, but researchers say it is the most accurate depiction of the creature yet.

item.title: Skull render of Muttaburrasaurus
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: WA Police stands by moderation strategy despite racist comments on social media posts
item.description: Racist comments are being published on WA Police social media posts with little moderation, but the force says it is not prepared to switch off comments.

item.title: Global tour operator Intrepid invests millions in Central Australia's iconic Ooraminna Homestead
item.description: Australian-based global tour operator Intrepid has announced a multi-million-dollar investment in Central Australia's Ooraminna Homestead, 30 kilometres south of Alice Springs.

item.title: Josh Hazlewood helps Royal Challengers Bengaluru to IPL win over Lucknow
item.description: Josh Hazlewood has produced another masterful display in the IPL to rocket Royal Challengers Bengaluru back to the top of the league.

item.title: Refinery blaze may impact Australia's petrol production 'for some time'
item.description: Energy Minister Chris Bowen says the fire in Geelong appears to be an accident at this stage and there are no suspicious circumstances.

item.title: Household spending up in March as fuel costs surge, over-65s lead spending growth, CBA data shows
item.description: Australians spent more in March as the outbreak of the Iran war drove up petrol prices. Older Australians increased their spending at the fastest pace, CBA data shows.

item.title: From a new Australian musical to buzzy horror, here's what's in cinemas in April
item.description: Plus a powerful look Palestinian life through multiple generations and a buzzy Japanese video game adaptation makes it to Australian shores.

item.title: WA wine industry warns of impact of Containers for Change expansion
item.description: A newly formed West Australian wine group warns that bottles of wine produced in the state could increase by $1.25 if the expansion of the Containers for Change scheme goes ahead.

item.title: Why Australia turned to Brunei to shore up supplies of critical crop fertiliser during Middle East war
item.description: The Middle East conflict has disrupted the flow of Australia's imports of the fertiliser urea. Facing the potential threat of reduced crop yields, Anthony Albanese looked to Australia's neighbours.

item.title: Court releases video showing scuttling of Maria Louise K
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: US jury finds Ticketmaster and Live Nation have anticompetitive monopoly
item.description: A New York jury finds concert giant Live Nation and its Ticketmaster subsidiary have a harmful monopoly over big concert venues.

item.title: Mother fears children may have been filmed with hidden cameras in Horsham Town Hall change rooms
item.description: A mother says she is disturbed after learning of hidden cameras found in the theatre change rooms her children used in November with a lack of dates adding to parents' concerns.

item.title: NSW nurses, midwives awarded major pay rise after decision from Industrial Relations Commission
item.description: Registered nurses will receive a "one-off reset" of a 10 per cent pay rise in the first year, backdated to July 1, 2025, and a 3 per cent pay rise the following two years.

item.title: Investigation launched into Papua New Guinea Defence Force recruitment processes following soldier protest
item.description: A small group of officers deserted their posts this week in protest over alleged government corruption.

item.title: Some country towns could lose more than half of their shade due to aging trees
item.description: Some country towns could lose up to 80 per cent of their shade due to aging trees, but replacing them is not simple for councils.

item.title: Son charged with mother's murder in Far North Queensland
item.description: The woman's body was found at an address in Mareeba in Far North Queensland.

item.title: Australia's rarest reptile, the kungaka skink, found in far west NSW national park
item.description: Not found anywhere else on Earth, a skink considered functionally extinct has been found in a national park in far west NSW.

item.title: Uncovered ballots prompt new count in seat of Narungga won by One Nation
item.description: The Electoral Commission of South Australia has uncovered more than 600 ballot papers that were not counted in the recent state election, including dozens from the closely contested seat of Narungga on the Yorke Peninsula.

item.title: Two Melbourne men found guilty of manslaughter over 1999 death of Canberra woman Irma Palasics
item.description: Two Melbourne men are found guilty of the manslaughter of Canberra woman Irma Palasics during a violent home invasion in 1999.

item.title: Unemployment rate remained at 4.3pc in March, in 'calm before the storm'
item.description: Australia's unemployment rate remained at 4.3 per cent in March, but economists say cracks could start appearing in coming months.

item.title: NRL six-again rule should be scrapped, according to coach Ricky Stuart
item.description: Canberra Raiders coach Ricky Stuart says the NRL has lost the fabric of its game, leading to anger over the league's controversial six-again rule change.

item.title: AFL Tribunal 'firestorm' taking heavy toll on players, says Saints coach Ross Lyon
item.description: St Kilda coach Ross Lyon says the damage from a "firestorm" around the AFL Tribunal is weighing heavily as his club signals it may appeal against Lance Collard's suspension.

item.title: Geelong fire could cause 'short-term petrol price shock' for Victoria
item.description: As Australians woke to the news of a major fire at the Geelong refinery — one of the nation's two remaining oil refineries — questions quickly turned to what this could mean for petrol prices.

item.title: Sunshine Coast massage therapist faces more sexual assault charges
item.description: A Queensland massage therapist arrested in February over the alleged sexual assault of four clients is facing 11 fresh charges after another four female clients came forward.

item.title: Man not criminally responsible for bashing and choking elderly grandmother in Sydney hospital
item.description: A man charged with choking and bashing an elderly woman at a Sydney hospital who later died has been found not criminally responsible due to a psychotic episode and likely schizophrenia.

item.title: Danny Gibson resigns from Launceston City Council after scandal-plagued term
item.description: The former mayor and councillor of Tasmanian's second-largest city resigns effective immediately, years after an ABC investigation uncovered historical allegations about his conduct towards young men in the theatre scene.

item.title: Farmers 'running on empty' welcome arrival of diesel to the Port of Newcastle
item.description: A massive crude oil tanker laden with diesel has docked in the Port of Newcastle to replenish regional New South Wales supplies, as the Middle East unrest continues.

item.title: More than 350 cannabis plants seized from Kalgoorlie home
item.description: A 53-year-old Kalgoorlie man is released on bail after the seizure of more than 350 cannabis plants from a property WA Police allege had been converted for the purpose of growing hydroponic cannabis.

item.title: LIV Golf's future looks bleak despite denials, with Saudi Arabia set to determine breakaway league's fate
item.description: Rumours of LIV Golf's immediate demise look wide of the mark, but the smoke is only growing thicker as denials from the league's CEO help paint a bleak picture.

item.title: Russia rains strikes across Ukraine; Kyiv is promised more drones
item.description: Russian forces have attacked the Ukrainian capital Kyiv and other cities, killing 17 people, as the defence leaders of more than 50 countries say they are aiming to send more aid to Ukraine.

item.title: Smoke to settle around Sydney from hazard reduction burns as firefighters seize favourable conditions
item.description: Smoky conditions across Greater Sydney are tipped to ease tomorrow as the wind picks up, after firefighters had a productive week burning hazards around Sydney's fringes.

item.title: NSW protest laws brought in after Bondi Beach attack deemed 'unconstitutional' by top court
item.description: The laws, rushed through parliament after the Bondi Beach terrorist attack, have been found to be unconstitutional by the state's highest court.

item.title: Australian dollar surges to four-year high as traders see Iran war ending in near-term
item.description: Analysts say the mood on global financial markets has improved considerably over the past few days.

item.title: Underworld figure Gavin Preston 'never' sat outside at cafe until day he was shot, court told
item.description: Victoria's Supreme Court is told underworld figure Gavin Preston was cautious about his surroundings and "never" sat outside a cafe he regularly attended — other than the day he was shot dead.

item.title: Administrators tell Liberty Bell Bay workers to take leave without pay or be sacked
item.description: About 175 workers at Australia's only manganese smelter, Liberty Bell Bay, have been told they will be laid off at the end of next week unless they take leave without pay.

item.title: Robot surprises Greens Senator David Shoebridge
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Hanson flattered by Coalition plan, defence gets a boost
item.description: This week in Canberra, Pauline Hanson learned imitation is the sincerest form of flattery, the defence budget was on the agenda and a fire threw the fuel crisis into sharp relief.

item.title: PM confirms purchase of 100m litres of diesel in bid to boost supply amid Iran war oil crisis
item.description: Anthony Albanese says that new powers allowing the federal government to underwrite the delivery of additional fuel cargoes have been used to purchase 100 million litres of diesel.

item.title: How to tell if your dog is in pain
item.description: New research suggests that many dog owners are not skilled in recognising pain in their pets.

item.title: Palestinians fear Gaza is being forgotten as Iran war drags on
item.description: Palestinians in Gaza fear they are being forgotten as the Iran war drags on, with Israeli strikes continuing despite a ceasefire and the humanitarian situation still dire.

item.title: Richard Marles defends US alliance, rules-based order amid push for more self-reliant defence force
item.description: Defence Minister Richard Marles has declared the United States alliance will remain indispensable for Australian national security even while Australia looks to build a more self-reliant defence force.

item.title: AFL Tribunal process 'too adversarial', Port Adelaide chief executive says
item.description: Port Adelaide's chief executive has slammed the AFL Tribunal process, labelling it "too legalistic" and "too adversarial".

item.title: Manta rays have been spotted in Darwin Harbour
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Swimmers patting a turtle
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: New report finds Darwin Harbour biodiversity, rare species rival those of famous Ningaloo Reef
item.description: New research has revealed Darwin Harbour's biodiversity, including its wide range of rare species, rivals that of the World Heritage-protected Ningaloo Reef.

item.title: Dylan de Prada refused bail, alleged Wangaratta stabbing victim in critical condition with severed ear
item.description: A man has been refused bail over an alleged stabbing incident that has left a 53-year-old woman in a critical condition with skull fractures and part of her ear severed.

item.title: Daicos inspires Magpies to win as Blues youngster misses kick after siren
item.description: Nick Daicos stars in the final quarter to inspire Collingwood to a win as Carlton youngster Talor Byrne misses a kick after the siren to tie the scores.

item.title: Thursday finance with David Chau
item.description: News from the world of finance and business. Top stories in text, video and audio. Business video news bulletin. Features and analysis.

item.title: Accused hacker allegedly targeted government departments, courthouse and gym, court hears
item.description: A 22-year-old Adelaide man who allegedly went on a "cybercrime spree", targeting several government departments and a courthouse, will remain behind bars until at the least the end of the year.

item.title: Trbojevic injures hamstring as Manly thrash Cowboys
item.description: The Sea Eagles have smashed North Queensland but the win has come at a heavy cost following a hamstring injury to star fullback Tom Trbojevic.

item.title: Laura Tingle sits down with Prime Minister Anthony Albanese to talk fuel security
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Pope Leo blasts 'tyrants' following public disagreement with Donald Trump over Iran war
item.description: The US-born pope has praised peace movements and peace makers during his visit to the African nation of Cameroon as the rift with US President Donald Trump intensifies.

item.title: Iran declares the Strait of Hormuz open, thousands of Lebanese return home — as it happened
item.description: Iran to reopen Strait of Hormuz, as thousands of displaced people in Lebanon are returning to their homes following a 10-day ceasefire deal with Israel.

item.title: Peter Mandelson failed vetting but was given UK ambassador to US job anyway
item.description: Peter Mandelson was sacked from the role after revelations about his connections with the late sex offender became public.

item.title: Donald Trump announces 10-day ceasefire between Israel and Lebanon
item.description: The US president says he "had excellent conversations" with Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu and Lebanese President Joseph Aoun.

item.title: Photographers and artists showcase world of fungi in exhibit at WAMA
item.description: An exhibition bridges the gap between art and science by inviting visitors to imagine the world through the senses of other species and consider our own fragile relationship with nature.

item.title: Weekly news quiz: Trump's AI image, a 200m record and the Artemis II splashdown
item.description: It's Friday, and that means it's time to test your news knowledge from the past seven days. Were you paying attention this week?

item.title: Why invasive plants can harm your garden and what you can grow instead
item.description: Agapanthus, morning glory or gazanias might be easy-growing plants in your home but their invasive nature can damage your garden and the environment. Here's what experts recommend.

item.title: Consumers are cutting back on spending amid higher risk of global recession
item.description: As fuel costs surge and supply fears grow, businesses and households say the economic shock from the war in the Middle East is already hitting home.

item.title: Australia on the verge of an aged care AI boom but experts warn of high risks
item.description: Artificial intelligence-enabled robot companions, personal behaviour monitors and pain management apps could soon be used more widely within the sector.

item.title: In charts: A look at Australia's record spending on defence
item.description: The Defence Department reveals where the money will be going as Australia plans to spend a record $425 billion over the next decade.

item.title: Anthony Albanese braces for economic consequences from protracted Strait of Hormuz blockade
item.description: Prime Minister Anthony Albanese insists Australia's sanctions on Russia and Iran will remain in place as the world scrambles to secure oil supplies amid a global crisis triggered by the Strait of Hormuz blockade.

item.title: Family of 13 becomes homeless amid rental shortage, highlighting national crisis
item.description: Finding a home for a large family in a regional area has left one family camping on the side of the highway in rural New South Wales. Nationally, Indigenous people are over-represented in homelessness statistics.

item.title: French president calls for under-15s social media ban, urges children to switch off and read
item.description: Emmanuel Macron calls for a social media ban for children under 15, a monthly screen-free day for all, and urges high school students to switch off their phones.

item.title: AFL-backed helmet company GameGear erases concussion protection claims
item.description: An AFL-endorsed helmet company has backtracked on its claims of "superior protection against concussion" and the league is now also facing a complaint lodged with the Australian Competition and Consumer Commission on the matter.

item.title: Sunshine in south-east Queensland to round out school holidays, showers in parts of north
item.description: The south-east corner will see slightly warmer temperatures and sunshine until Sunday, however, localised heavy falls are on the cards for parts of the central interior and north-west.

item.title: Myanmar's coup leader seeks legitimacy as family wealth surfaces in Thailand
item.description: As the Myanmar military carried out air strikes during a brutal civil war, the junta chief's family quietly purchased luxury real estate in Thailand.

item.title: Coca-Cola suggests it could abandon Queensland Containers for Change scheme amid proposed reforms
item.description: The Queensland government recently introduced proposed laws to overhaul the governance of the scheme, while also ruling out an increase to the 10-cent refund amount.

item.title: Iran briefing with Matthew Doran: 10-day ceasefire in Lebanon begins without Israeli withdrawal
item.description: Israel and Lebanon agree to a 10-day ceasefire, but Israel's military will remain on Lebanese territory, meaning Hezbollah's conditions have not been met.

item.title: Ben Roberts-Smith expected to apply for bail on war crimes charges
item.description: Australia's most decorated living soldier has spent more than a week in custody. Today in court, he is expected to make a bid to be released on bail after being charged with war crimes.

item.title: Migration debate deserves better policy approach and less politicking from Liberals
item.description: The Liberals need to resolve the internal ambiguities they have on big policy questions when it comes to migration.

item.title: BreastScreen SA did not get ethics approval or consent before research, ombudsman finds
item.description: South Australia's public breast cancer screening service conducted research without ethics approval or consent from almost 100 patients, prompting the service to "unreservedly" apologise.

item.title: Friday fuel filter: Refining the week's fuel news so you don't have to
item.description: We know you have to get where you're going this weekend. ABC News is here to help with a round-up of all the key news on fuel price and supply in Australia.

item.title: Socceroos to play friendly against Switzerland before FIFA World Cup opener
item.description: The Socceroos will conclude their preparations for the World Cup in North America with a warm-up friendly against Switzerland, the first time they will have faced European opposition since 2023.

item.title: Damages, withdrawn charges being explored in wake of struck out controversial NSW protest laws
item.description: Protesters caught up in the government's now-canned protest laws could seek damages as lawyers review the implications of the Supreme Court ruling them "unconstitutional".

item.title: Gold Coast holiday park residents facing eviction to accommodate tourists
item.description: Time is running out for residents facing eviction from Kirra Beach Tourist Park. In 2018, the council told them they needed to move out by 2028 to make way for tourists, but with rising rent and property prices, they say they cannot afford to live anywhere else.

item.title: WA state guardianship and administration to be investigated in parliamentary inquiry
item.description: The state agencies which control the lives of some of Western Australia's most vulnerable people are set to be scrutinised in a parliamentary inquiry, with public submissions now open.

item.title: Carlton confirms Elijah Hollands suffered 'mental health episode' against Collingwood
item.description: Carlton provides an update on Elijah Hollands's wellbeing after the 23-year-old's performance against Collingwood attracts widespread concern.

item.title: Donald Trump repeats he is 'not happy with Australia' as Strait of Hormuz crisis continues
item.description: Donald Trump again accuses Australia of refusing to help the US in the Strait of Hormuz, but the Australian government maintains there has been no formal request from the US.

item.title: Bank warns of significant surge in inflation as oil price spikes — as it happened
item.description: The Royal Bank of Canada (RBC) expects Australian inflation to surge to 6 per cent, while adding another RBA interest rate hike to its forecast.

item.title: The journey of Aled Jones from a boy soprano to consummate artist
item.description: From learning Walking in the Air on the fly to working with Leonard Bernstein, Aled Jones is currently touring Australia and shares some of his most colourful stories.

item.title: Broome rapist remains on bail for five months despite guilty plea
item.description: Wayne Jeffrey Doherty remained in the Broome community for five months after pleading guilty to rape.

item.title: French Open bans cameras in player areas after Melbourne criticism
item.description: Organisers of the French Open have promised that their players will enjoy camera-free privacy in a space of their own, following criticism the Australian Open copped from top stars this year.

item.title: Kulbanyi bush tea from NT station hits the shelves in Asia
item.description: The product is being sent from a remote property in the Northern Territory all the way to Japan, Korea and China.

item.title: Mining magnate Andrew Forrest takes Meta to court over scam ads using his likeness
item.description: Australian billionaire mining magnate Andrew Forrest is asking a US federal court in Silicon Valley to hold Meta accountable for scam ads using his likeness without permission.

item.title: Artemis II astronauts praise their moonship's performance, especially the heat shield
item.description: The astronauts say their lunar flyby puts NASA in a much better position for a crewed Moon landing in two years and an eventual Moon base.

item.title: Endangered southern brown bandicoots bounce back at Brown Hill Creek
item.description: A small colony of endangered brown bandicoots just south of Adelaide continue to breed as conservationists hope to connect them with other populations.

item.title: Southern brown bandicoot filmed at Brownhill Creek Recreation Park
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Iranian footballers who sought asylum in Australia 'overwhelmed' by support
item.description: Two Iranian footballers given refuge in Australia after the AFC Women's Asian Cup say they are focused on their health, safety and rebuilding their lives.

item.title: Refinery blaze will not bump Australia to stage three of national fuel response
item.description: Anthony Albanese has cut a trip to Malaysia short to visit Viva Energy Refinery in Geelong, which went up in flames late on Wednesday.

item.title: Sydney childcare worker hit with 129 additional charges for using children to make abuse material
item.description: A Sydney childcare worker accused of using children to make abuse material has been charged with 129 new offences.

item.title: Former Austria goalkeeper Alexander Manninger killed in Salzburg after car hit by train
item.description: Former Austria goalkeeper Alexander Manninger, who played for Arsenal and a string of Italian clubs, died on Thursday at the age of 48 after the car he was driving was hit by a train at a crossing near Salzburg.

item.title: Acting US Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) head Todd Lyons to leave agency
item.description: Todd Lyons' last day is set for May 31 and he will move to the private sector, Department of Homeland security secretary Markwayne Mullin said in a statement.

item.title: Jet fuel shortage could see major flight disruptions in Europe, price hikes and cancellations globally
item.description: Europe could experience major flight disruptions within weeks as global energy officials warn the continent has "maybe six weeks of jet fuel left", which could lead to further price hikes and cancellations for Australian travellers.

item.title: Before the timer reaches zero, 100 bombs have dropped
item.description: The Israel Defense Forces labelled it Operation Eternal Darkness. Inside Lebanon, it's now called Black Wednesday. ABC NEWS Verify has geolocated the explosions across 10 minutes of chaos.

item.title: Former soldier Ben Roberts-Smith granted bail over alleged war crime of murder offences
item.description: Ben Roberts-Smith was granted bail after spending a week in custody over alleged war crime of murder offences.

item.title: St Kilda to appeal suspension of Lance Collard for using homophobic slur in VFL match
item.description: St Kilda is appealing the nine-match ban given to Lance Collard for uttering a homophobic comment, saying the young forward maintains his innocence.

item.title: By-election called for seat of Stafford in Brisbane's north after Jimmy Sullivan's death
item.description: Former Member for Stafford Jimmy Sullivan was found dead at his home earlier this month, after police were called to reports of a non-suspicious death.

item.title: Prince Harry and Meghan meet Bondi terror attack survivors during Sydney leg of Australian tour
item.description: The Duke and Duchess of Sussex spoke with lifeguards who responded to the December 14 Bondi Beach terror attack and survivors.

item.title: National Indigenous Cricket Championships offers pathways for young players
item.description: The National Indigenous Cricket Championships, held in Mackay for the second year, has become one of the key development pathways for young talent.

item.title: Harry and Meghan take Sydney Harbour yacht trip for Invictus foundation
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Narungga votes that went missing see margin increase for One Nation
item.description: One Nation has retained the South Australian seat of Narungga, following a count of ballots that were missed on election day.

item.title: Mayor fears hidden cameras may have been in Horsham Town Hall change rooms for years
item.description: The Horsham mayor is worried that hidden cameras reportedly found in the town hall theatre change rooms could have been there for years.

item.title: Keir Starmer looks to Europe as war in Iran further fractures US relationship
item.description: Keir Starmer was never a fan of Brexit. Now, under the cover of the war in Iran and a fractured US relationship, he's looking to Europe to return Britain to the fold.

item.title: Kununurra and Newman set for tougher liquor restrictions amid soaring violence
item.description: West Australian Racing and Gaming Minister Paul Papalia says Newman and Kununurra are in for tougher measures, including closing bottle shops for two days each week.

item.title: Device found near Bali and Lombok identified as Chinese undersea monitoring system
item.description: A large torpedo-shaped device discovered near an island in a strategically important strait between Bali and Lombok has been identified by defence analysts as a Chinese undersea monitoring system.

item.title: 'Too big to ignore' housing problem top priority for productivity commissioner
item.description: Productivity Commissioner Danielle Wood says Australia will not meet the federal government's 1.2 million new home target, as residential construction rates fall.

item.title: People returning to Nabatiyeh in Lebanon after ceasefire
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Makers of As Deep as the Grave defend use of AI-generated Val Kilmer in movie
item.description: A trailer for a film with an AI-generated character in the likeness of the late Val Kilmer has been slammed by social media users, but the filmmakers are defending their work.

item.title: Victorian political donations website goes dark after High Court struck out election law
item.description: Victoria has been left without laws to govern party donation disclosures — exposing the state's politics to a flood of unchecked donations.

item.title: Gout Gout targets sub-10 run in 100m at Australian Athletics Junior Championships
item.description: Gout Gout fires a warning shot back at Justin Gatlin for disparaging his national 200 metres record, as he sets his sights on another feat in his young career.

item.title: NSW prisoner Kyle Quayle back in jail after accidental release
item.description: A prisoner who was mistakenly released from a northern New South Wales prison this week has been returned to jail to complete his sentence.

item.title: Government advisory group recommends delaying NDIS 'robo-planning' changes
item.description: The federal government has been urged by its own NDIS advisory group to delay the rollout of a controversial new way of determining support plans for participants.

item.title: Mental health nurse Adam McGovern jailed for sexually abusing 14-year-old patient
item.description: A former West Australian mental health nurse is sentenced to 12 years in jail after being convicted for persistent sexual abuse of a teenager and making "upskirting" videos of women and children.

item.title: Liberty Bell Bay smelter workers plead for government support as final payday looms
item.description: Tasmanian smelter workers at risk of losing their salaries within the next two weeks are pleading with the state and federal governments to step in — pointing to large bailouts given to other companies.

item.title: Viva Geelong refinery fire won't be a problem for us, Tasmanian government says
item.description: Tasmania has 100 days of petrol and 58 days of diesel supply available, the government says — and that the Geelong refinery fire won't be a problem.

item.title: Panthers win golden point thriller but lose key man Kenny
item.description: Penrith have paid a heavy price for their extra time win over the Dolphins with hooker Mitch Kenny facing a lengthy stint on the sidelines.

item.title: Pacific leaders declare emergency over potential fuel shortages
item.description: The prime minister of Solomon Islands says Pacific leaders have declared an emergency over the looming threat of fuel shortages, while the Australian government contemplates a plan which could see stocks of diesel held by the US military sent to Pacific nations suffering shortages.

item.title: Daughter witnessed Maree Mavis Crabtree crush drugs into smoothie that allegedly killed son Jonathan, jury hears
item.description: The daughter of a Gold Coast woman accused of killing her son alleges she saw her mum use a hammer to crush up drugs before putting them into a lethal fruit smoothie.

item.title: Albanese joins meeting of leaders to talk about reopening the Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Anthony Albanese has joined a virtual meeting of world leaders to discuss how the Strait of Hormuz can be secured after the conflict in the Middle East ends.

item.title: Supreme Court hears murder accused Andrew Mungatopi called for help after stabbing sistergirl
item.description: The partner of a man charged with the stabbing murder of a Tiwi Islands sistergirl tells the NT Supreme Court she saw him run outside "like a flash" before crying and repeatedly calling out "call the police".

item.title: ABC News - Trusted Source of Latest News & Headlines
item.description: News from the world of finance and business. Top stories in text, video and audio. Business video news bulletin. Features and analysis.

item.title: Bulldogs cop injury carnage on 'traumatic' night as superstar Darcy tears ACL
item.description: The Bulldogs are trounced by 75 points and lose Sam Darcy to a knee injury as Sydney retains its supremacy against GWS.

item.title: Cigno Australia, BSF Solutions and directors fined $7 million over payday lending scheme
item.description: The fines were issued against an operation that breached credit rules and generated more than $91 million in fees and charges.

item.title: Ben Roberts-Smith leaves Silverwater jail after securing bail
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Former Darwin Middle School teacher Darroch Robinson sentenced to prison for striking student
item.description: The court heard Darroch Robinson struck the girl after she insulted him following the confiscation of her phone, causing her to fall into a table and break her teeth.

item.title: Parents of Aboriginal teen share anguish during sentence hearing of convicted police officer
item.description: The parents of 16-year-old Jai Kalani Wright, who died after colliding with a police car, share their emotional testimonies in court as the sentencing hearing begins.

item.title: Aung San Suu Kyi to be freed earlier as Myanmar releases ex-president Win Myint from jail
item.description: Myanmar's ex-leader Aung San Suu Kyi has her prison sentence cut as thousands of prisoners are freed. Meanwhile, the UN reveals 2025 marked the deadliest year on record for Rohingya refugees fleeing Myanmar.

item.title: Iran declares Strait of Hormuz open as Lebanon ceasefire begins
item.description: US President Donald Trump immediately welcomed the news from Tehran but went on to warn that the US blockade of Iran's ports would continue.

item.title: Oil prices plunge after news Strait of Hormuz to open
item.description: Oil prices plunged more than 10 per cent after Iran declared the Strait of Hormuz would be "completely open" but shipping giants are approaching the situation with caution.

item.title: Millennials and gen Z clash in season two of Beef, starring Carey Mulligan and Oscar Isaac
item.description: The dark comedy series returns with a biting look at generational conflict, starring Carey Mulligan and Oscar Isaac as a "combustible" middle-aged couple.

item.title: Royal tour to brand tour: Harry and Meghan seek duty and dollars in Australia
item.description: Mixing philanthropy, influencing and brand building, Harry and Meghan’s Australian tour revives a question Queen Elizabeth once shut down: can the 'half in, half out' royal life work?

item.title: As Iran war fallout spreads, the focus shifts to Trump's mind — as it should
item.description: The world is stuck watching a performative series of announcements about meetings that could, may, will happen, involving a president not dealing with the reality that he is in a weak and confused bargaining position.

item.title: What to watch, listen and make this weekend: The Boys, Jessie and sausage rolls
item.description: Our critics' picks for your weekend entertainment from the small screen to the kitchen oven.

item.title: ABC 50/50 quiz: This week we're testing your knowledge of the Big Day Out, footy fairytales and the circles of hell
item.description: Every question has only two options: one is right, and the other is wrong. You have an even chance of guessing correctly each time.

item.title: Iran war and energy shock make delivering the federal budget unusually hard
item.description: The budget's usual timing on the second Tuesday of May is an artifice of political convention. This year's edition just happens to be coinciding with the thunderclap of history in flux.

item.title: Middle East conflict creates volatile airfare market as prices fluctuate
item.description: Australians are finding domestic flights for as little as $65 on some routes, while others are paying thousands to travel interstate as airlines quietly reshape the market in response to conflict in the Middle East.

item.title: Video of police officer restraining 13yo girl on Perth train sparks excessive force probe
item.description: Video of an off-duty police officer restraining a 13yo girl on a Perth train has sparked accusations of excessive force.

item.title: Why global stocks keep surging despite economic impact of Iran war
item.description: The Australian Securities Exchange is within striking distance of new records as economists issue worst-case warnings of a close call for a global recession.

item.title: Labor considering ways to spare new homes from capital gains tax changes
item.description: The possible budget centrepiece is likely to be finalised much closer to the May deadline than usual this year given global uncertainty, but progress has been made in recent weeks.

item.title: Australia banks on Ghost Bat and Ghost Shark in drone warfare shift
item.description: Australia is on a push to keep pace with the rapid evolution of drone warfare, with an emphasis on locally produced Ghost Bat and Ghost Shark systems.

item.title: Strait of Hormuz closes as US blockade continues: Iranian official — as it happened
item.description: Iran has labelled the ongoing US blockade of the strait as a violation of the ceasefire agreement, while Donald Trump says "very good conversations are going on" between the two countries.

item.title: Coorong fishers deploy 'ninja tactics' to outsmart fish-stealing seals
item.description: Commercial fishers question their future as they compete with ravenous long-nosed seals that continue to rip man-sized holes in fish nets in South Australia's Coorong.

item.title: Lone dingo 'Derek' a concern for some as he roams suburbs in Alice Springs
item.description: An lone dingo, affectionately known as Derek, has been spotted in suburban streets, backyards and even in dog parks, raising some concern for Alice Springs residents.

item.title: 'Derek' the dingo spotted roaming in Eastside
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Calls for more awareness of functional neurological disorder to reduce diagnosis delays
item.description: People with functional neurological disorder say they experience misdiagnosis and struggles within the medical system.

item.title: Ben Roberts-Smith prosecution for alleged war crimes complex, legal expert says
item.description: A legal expert says the criminal prosecution of former soldier Ben Roberts-Smith will test Australia's war crimes legal framework in ways never seen before, as the case moves toward a trial.

item.title: How artificial intelligence is changing business interaction with customers
item.description: Businesses say they are using AI to speed up service delivery and improve the customer experience, but economists warn this may be the "calm before the storm".

item.title: Missing votes bungle caps off disastrous poll for SA's electoral commission
item.description: The 2026 state election has been a reputational disaster for South Australia's electoral authority. Its blunder in Narungga was the "cherry on top".

item.title: 'High-octane world' of drifting told through orchestral music in Adelaide
item.description: Motorsport and orchestral music are coming together in a premiere in Adelaide this weekend.

item.title: An executive at FIFA's new betting partner settled an insider-trading lawsuit
item.description: The backstory of the prediction market industry's newest entrant has done little to calm concerns as it partners with several giants of sports broadcasting.

item.title: Chris Minns considers his options after court ruling over NSW protest laws
item.description: NSW Premier Chris Minns must now decide whether to proceed with the next restrictions he hoped to impose on protests after his latest court defeat.

item.title: How one walk could spark a national conversation on Australia's past
item.description: Indigenous leader Travis Lovett sets off on a trek to Canberra, calling for action from the federal government to support a national truth-telling process for First Nations people.

item.title: Victoria's Upper Murray region has a history of hiding gun-toting fugitives in its rugged hills
item.description: Dezi Freeman used Victoria's Upper Murray region to hide, but it has long been a popular location for those trying to avoid capture.

item.title: Lime scooters and e-bikes have arrived in Canberra. Here's what that means for commuters
item.description: With Neuron's orange scooters having left Canberra, Lime has introduced a fleet of green scooters and e-bikes across the capital in partnership with the ACT government.

item.title: Frustrated parents of under-16s look for help to enforce 'ineffective' social media ban
item.description: Tech companies are coming under fire as Australian parents increasingly voice concerns that some online platforms are failing to keep kids under 16 off their platforms.

item.title: Despite the promise of NBN, thousands of Australians still have no access and 'patchy' internet
item.description: Despite popular belief, not all Australians are guaranteed access to the NBN — and as many consumers have discovered, alternative networks do not always provide quality service.

item.title: Producer backs pitch for major film studio in Port Kembla
item.description: As the New South Wales government searches for a site for a second Sydney film studio, one of Australia's most prominent producers suggests a regional location is the ideal fit.

item.title: FIFA World Cup fans set for $200 train tickets for 15 minute trip to MetLife Stadium
item.description: A 15-minute round-trip train fare from New York to New Jersey's MetLife Stadium will set World Cup football fans back more than $200 as officials struggle to cover rising costs.

item.title: Do acupuncture and herbs actually help with menopause symptoms?
item.description: Complementary therapies may help some menopausal symptoms, but more high-quality research is needed to understand how effective and safe these treatments truly are.

item.title: Search underway for man missing from cruise ship off Brisbane coast
item.description: Carnival Cruise Line has confirmed it is assisting authorities in a search for a missing man.

item.title: How the daughters of WA mining pioneers came to be opponents in high-profile legal fight
item.description: Their fathers were school buddies turned mining pioneers, and in another life they could have been friends — but Gina Rinehart and Angela Bennett came to be opponents in one of the most high-stakes legal battles Australia has ever seen.

item.title: Marie-Louise Eta takes charge of Union Berlin as first female head coach in Europe's big five leagues
item.description: Amid a storm of misogynistic criticism, Marie-Louise Eta will become the first woman to take charge of a men's football team in one of Europe's top five leagues. But she just wants everyone to focus on the football.

item.title: The ancient origins behind wreath laying on Anzac Day
item.description: A remembrance tradition that Australians have adapted to commemorate fallen soldiers has ancient Greek and Roman origins.

item.title: Fremantle Prison break remembered 150 years on as 'ultimate story' of hope and freedom
item.description: The story of how a Fenian captain from Massachusetts defied the odds and rescued six Irish political convicts from a prison on the very edge of the world.

item.title: Social croquet connects communities as clubs record jump in numbers
item.description: Golf croquet helps isolated elderly people reconnect with others through social competition, morning tea and laughs.

item.title: CCTV captures drugs being moved in Fiji
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: David Wright and Tui tell how a charter boat booking became a drug smuggling run
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Agricultural, transport industries call for urgent road repairs in flood-hit Northern Territory
item.description: Workers in road-reliant industries are calling on the federal government to provide urgent funding to repair the NT's road network, which was severely damaged by floods.

item.title: LIV Golf facing funding fight to 'keep us going', CEO Scott O'Neil admits in deleted interview
item.description: The future of LIV Golf remains in doubt as chief executive Scott O'Neil admits in an interview that the league is "funded through the season" but work is required beyond that "to keep us going".

item.title: Death of Sandipan Dhar at Joondalup Health Campus was 'probably preventable'
item.description: The death of a toddler at one of Perth's major hospitals was "probably preventable" and the failure to perform a blood test on him was "more than a missed opportunity", a coroner finds.

item.title: Generation alpha's fascination with old technology on show at Denmark Youth Festival
item.description: With the constant evolution of the digital world, community history is rapidly disappearing into memories and museums. But a historical society on WA's south coast is delighting today's kids by showing them the technology older Australians grew up with.

item.title: How Maltese migrants united to shape Mackay's sugarcane legacy
item.description: Long before they were cast in bronze, a group of Maltese migrants quietly reshaped who would own farmland across north Queensland.

item.title: Skip the takeaway. This halloumi wrap is quicker and healthy too - ABC News
item.description: The only cooking in this simple dinner is the halloumi. It has takeaway vibes and just the right balance of flavours to become a family favourite.

item.title: The men tricked into helping drug smugglers import Fiji's largest meth haul
item.description: Two men have told Foreign Correspondent how they were unwittingly drawn into Fiji's largest-ever meth importation.

item.title: Crows sensationally down Saints after emotional scenes at Adelaide Oval
item.description: The Crows hold on by one point in a thriller at Adelaide Oval, as the club pays tribute to grieving skipper Jordan Dawson.

item.title: Indemnified soldiers give evidence in Ben Roberts-Smith case, documents show
item.description: Ben Roberts-Smith was on Friday granted bail in the NSW Local Court, with his case over war crimes charges expected to return to court in early June.

item.title: India's parliament rejects move to increase number of women in office
item.description: The largest democracy in the world has rejected a move to increase the number of women in parliament. What could have been a 'beacon' for global women's rights became marred by controversy.

item.title: Women's boxing on the rise but fighters still struggle to find opponents, finance fights
item.description: Jess Messina is an Australian champion but she has dropped a weight class to find a fight and struggles to find promoters prepared to back her financially.

item.title: Haas expected to miss several weeks as Broncos count cost of brave win
item.description: Brisbane's horror run of injuries has continued with Payne Haas limping off in the first half of the Broncos' one-point win over Wests Tigers.

item.title: Trump says ceasefire with Iran could end without a long-term deal
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Mushroom murderer's estranged husband Simon Patterson writing memoir
item.description: Simon Patterson, who claims his murderous ex-wife tried to poison him on several occasions, is pursuing a book deal, his media manager has announced.

item.title: Australian tea tree oil industry faces uncertainty over EU ban proposal
item.description: The EU is considering banning Australian tea tree oil after a study raised concerns about potential reproductive risks, a move industry leaders warn could devastate exports and trigger global impacts.

item.title: Gout Gout wins another under-20 100m title at Australian Athletics Junior Championships
item.description: Gout Gout waves goodbye to his opponents en route to another national under-20 title in the 100m, as the wait goes on for him to dip below the 10-second barrier.

item.title: Power price hikes threaten Northern Territory councils, may cut access to services like pools and street lights
item.description: Power price hikes are expected to be passed onto local councils by the Northern Territory government in the coming month, with Darwin's lord mayor warning some community services could be cut as a result.

item.title: Traffic map appears to show ships passing through Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Dowerin buzzing as Lance 'Buddy' Franklin returns to footy for childhood club
item.description: Lance Franklin has pulled on the boots to help the Dowerin Tigers, where he played his formative footy, raise much-needed funds for lights and facilities.

item.title: Protesters arrested at Brisbane pro-Palestine rally featuring banned phrase
item.description: Protesters have been arrested for chanting and displaying a phrase banned in Queensland at a rally in Brisbane on Saturday.

item.title: Alleged arson attacks and shootings in Sydney prompt fears of escalating violence
item.description: NSW Police has expressed concern about escalating violence between Sydney's organised crime networks, following several alleged arson attacks and public shootings overnight.

item.title: CCTV captures machete attack at popular Melbourne shopping strip
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Stephen Dziedzic in Melbourne where Richard Marles met with Japanese defence minister
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Convoy of tankers seen leaving Strait of Hormuz on tracking map
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Wallaroos beaten by USA in Pacific Four Series after Kansas City storm delay
item.description: Australia loses 33-12 to the USA Eagles in their Pacific Four Series after storms in Kansas City delay kick-off by almost three hours.

item.title: Labor plots political comeback in Tasmania at first conference since 2019
item.description: Rank and file members, unionists, party elders, and MPs gathered for Tasmanian Labor's first state conference in seven years, as the party looks to plot its way out of the political wilderness.

item.title: Supernova Comic Con attendee killed after car strikes pedestrians in Melbourne
item.description: A young man who was killed when a car struck a group of pedestrians in Melbourne's north-west was on his way home from attending the Supernova Comic Con & Gaming convention, police say.

item.title: Japan, Australia sign deal for first three Mogami frigate warships
item.description: Defence Minister Richard Marles and his Japanese counterpart, Koizumi Shinjirō, signed the deal aboard one of the warships docked in Melbourne.
